mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 7.0 percent, consumer spending growth collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, triggering a sustained sell-off in equity markets over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold fails to separate bull and bear periods with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in consumer credit availability and housing starts signals robust household balance sheet repair and capital expenditure, driving a multi-month rally in equity markets as aggregate demand accelerates.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative 60-day forward return in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during an economic expansion reflect improving factory utilization and supply chain normalization, which generates short-term positive momentum in equity prices over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative 5-day returns during expansion phases, or the directional accuracy drops below 50% when filtered to non-recessionary macro environments.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate a growing labor force and expanding wage base, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS levels remains negative or near zero across all 3-year rolling windows, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with near-zero policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses real discount rates and corporate margins, forcing equity valuations lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL is high and FEDFUNDS is low, or the combined proxy yields a positive 20-day return in > 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 2.0 percent annualized, corporate earnings momentum accelerates and investor risk appetite expands, generating a sustained positive equity trend over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns are negative in > 55% of periods where the GDP threshold is met, or the threshold condition shows no predictive power in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in total payrolls during a recession signals deepening labor market deterioration, which triggers aggressive portfolio de-risking and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal predicts positive 20-day returns during recessionary periods, or the interaction effect loses predictive accuracy (directional accuracy < 45%) when filtered to NBER-defined recessions.
